project_specification.overview: A web application that lets home cooks save, organize, and scale recipes. Users upload recipes manually or import from URLs, tag by cuisine and diet, and adjust serving sizes with automatic ingredient scaling.
project_specification.target_audience: Home cooks and meal planners who want a personal recipe library that's faster to search than bookmarks and more flexible than printed cookbooks.

frontend.state_management: React Query for server state; React hooks for local state

database.dev: SQLite (via env toggle for local development)

project_specification.prerequisites: - Python 3.12+ with uv installed - Node.js 18+ for frontend - Environment variables: DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET

core_features.category: - User can register with email and password (returns 201 with user_id) - System validates email format (RFC 5322) and rejects duplicates with 409 error - System validates password minimum 8 characters with at least one number - System hashes passwords using bcrypt before storing in database - User can login and receive JWT access_token (15 min) and refresh_token (7 day) - System returns 401 with "Invalid credentials" on failed login - User can logout and system invalidates refresh_token in database - System auto-refreshes access_token silently using refresh_token when it expires - System returns 401 on all protected endpoints when token is missing or expired - User can view their profile: display name, email, account creation date - User can update their display name from the profile settings page - User can change password by providing current password and new password - System rejects password change if current password is incorrect with 400 error

- User can create a recipe with title, description, servings, prep time, cook time - User can add ingredients to a recipe as a list of { name, quantity, unit } objects - User can add step-by-step instructions to a recipe - User can edit any field of an existing recipe - User can delete a recipe (soft delete — sets deleted_at) - System returns 403 if user tries to access another user's recipe - User can upload a cover photo for a recipe (JPG, PNG, WEBP; max 5MB) - System resizes uploaded images to max 1200px on the longest side - User can duplicate an existing recipe to use it as a starting point - System tracks created_at and updated_at timestamps for every recipe

- User can adjust the serving size on the recipe detail page using a number input - System scales all ingredient quantities proportionally when serving size changes - System rounds scaled quantities to sensible precision (e.g., 0.5 not 0.4999) - System preserves original serving size and quantities separately for reset - User can click "Reset" to restore the original serving size and quantities - Scaling state is local to the session and not persisted to the database

- User can add free-form tags to any recipe (e.g., "vegan", "quick", "italian") - System shows autocomplete suggestions for previously used tags while typing - User can remove a tag by clicking the X on the tag chip - User can filter the recipe list by one or more tags (AND logic) - System shows all unique tags used by the user in the settings page - User can rename a tag globally (renames it on all recipes that use it) - User can mark any recipe as a favourite with a star icon - User can filter the recipe list to show only favourites - System shows recipe count next to each tag in the settings page

- User sees the dashboard at /dashboard showing all their recipes as a grid of cards - Each recipe card shows: cover photo thumbnail, title, tags, prep+cook time, servings - System fetches recipes sorted by updated_at descending by default - User can switch between grid view and list view with a toggle button - System shows 24 recipes per page with pagination controls at the bottom - User can search recipes by title using a search input in the toolbar (debounced 300ms) - User can filter recipes by tag using a multi-select dropdown - User can filter recipes to show only favourites using a heart toggle button - Active filters are shown as removable chips in the toolbar - User can clear all filters with a single "Clear filters" button - System shows total count of recipes matching current filters - System shows empty state with illustration and "Add your first recipe" CTA when no recipes exist - System shows "No results found" state with filter reset option when filters match nothing - Dashboard shows loading skeleton cards while fetching data - User can click any recipe card to navigate to the recipe detail page

- App is fully responsive on mobile (375px+), tablet (768px+), and desktop (1280px+) - Navigation sidebar is visible on desktop; collapses to hamburger menu on mobile - App uses skeleton screen loading states (not spinners) for list and detail pages - All async actions show inline loading indicators on the triggering button - All form validation errors appear inline below the relevant field - App shows success/error toast notifications in the top-right corner (auto-dismiss 4s) - All destructive actions require a confirmation dialog before executing - App supports dark mode via system preference with manual toggle in header - Dark mode preference is persisted in localStorage and applied on page load - Active filters and sort state are persisted in URL query parameters (shareable) - App supports full keyboard navigation: all interactive elements are focusable - App shows 404 error page for unknown routes with a link back to the dashboard - Page titles update to reflect the current route (e.g., "Pasta Carbonara | Recipe Manager")

- API returns consistent JSON envelope: { "data": ..., "error": null, "status": 200 } - API returns { "data": null, "error": { "message": "...", "code": "..." }, "status": 4xx } on errors - API validates all request bodies with Pydantic v2 and returns 400 with field-level errors - API returns 401 for all requests missing or with invalid/expired JWT token - API returns 403 if authenticated user attempts to access another user's resource - API returns 404 with descriptive message when a resource is not found - API returns 500 with unique error_id for unexpected server errors (logged server-side) - API enables CORS for the configured FRONTEND_URL environment variable - API returns paginated responses as: { "items": [...], "total": N, "page": 1, "per_page": 24, "has_next": true } - GET /api/recipes accepts query params: search, tag, favourites, sort_by, sort_dir, page, per_page

table.name: users
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()

table.name: refresh_tokens
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
expires_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL
created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()

table.name: recipes
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
INDEX idx_recipes_user_id ON recipes(user_id) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL

table.name: ingredients
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
recipe_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES recipes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

table.name: instructions
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
recipe_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES recipes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

table.name: recipe_tags
table.column: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
recipe_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES recipes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

api_endpoints_summary.domain: POST /api/auth/register - Register new user account POST /api/auth/login - Login and receive JWT tokens POST /api/auth/logout - Logout and invalidate refresh token POST /api/auth/refresh - Refresh access token using refresh token GET /api/auth/me - Get current user profile PATCH /api/auth/me - Update display name POST /api/auth/me/password - Change password

GET /api/recipes - List recipes with search, filters, and pagination POST /api/recipes - Create a new recipe GET /api/recipes/{id} - Get single recipe with ingredients, instructions, and tags PUT /api/recipes/{id} - Update recipe header fields DELETE /api/recipes/{id} - Soft-delete a recipe POST /api/recipes/{id}/duplicate - Duplicate a recipe
PUT /api/recipes/{id}/ingredients - Replace all ingredients for a recipe
PUT /api/recipes/{id}/instructions - Replace all instructions for a recipe
PUT /api/recipes/{id}/tags - Replace all tags for a recipe
GET /api/tags - Get all unique tags used by current user POST /api/tags/rename - Rename a tag globally across all recipes

ui_layout.structure:
Root layout: - Header bar (logo, "New Recipe" CTA, user avatar dropdown with Settings/Logout) - Left sidebar (desktop): Dashboard, Favourites, Tags, Settings navigation links - Main content area (full width on mobile, fills remaining space on desktop) - Toast notification portal (top-right)
Pages: / - Redirect to /dashboard if logged in, else /login /login - Email + password login form, link to register /register - Email + display name + password registration form /dashboard - Recipe grid with toolbar (search, tag filter, favourites toggle) /recipes/new - Create recipe form (title, description, servings, ingredients, steps) /recipes/[id] - Recipe detail: cover photo, ingredients, instructions, scaling control /recipes/[id]/edit - Edit recipe form /settings - Profile settings and password change /settings/tags - Tag management (rename, delete)

design_system.color_palette: Primary: #f97316 (orange-500) - buttons, links, active states Primary dark: #ea580c (orange-600) - hover states Success: #22c55e (green-500) - success toasts Danger: #ef4444 (red-500) - delete buttons, error messages Warning: #f59e0b (amber-500) - warning states Background: #ffffff (light) / #0f172a (dark) Surface: #f8fafc (light) / #1e293b (dark) Text: #0f172a (light) / #f1f5f9 (dark)
design_system.typography: Font family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif Headings: font-semibold, tracking-tight Body: font-normal, leading-relaxed Code: JetBrains Mono, monospace
design_system.animations: Transitions: 150-200ms ease Loading: skeleton screens (not spinners) Modals: fade + scale in Toasts: slide-in from right, fade-out on dismiss

key_interactions.interaction: 1. User clicks "New Recipe" in the header 2. Fills in title, description, servings, prep and cook time 3. Adds ingredients one by one (name, quantity, unit) 4. Adds step-by-step instructions 5. Uploads a cover photo (optional) 6. Clicks "Save Recipe" 7. System saves and redirects to the recipe detail page

1. User opens a recipe detail page 2. Sees the serving size control (e.g., "4 servings") 3. Changes serving size to 8 using the number input 4. System instantly scales all ingredient quantities proportionally 5. User clicks "Reset" to restore original quantities

1. User navigates to /dashboard 2. Types "pasta" in the search box — list filters to matching recipes 3. Selects "italian" from the tag filter dropdown 4. List narrows further to italian pasta recipes 5. User clicks "Export" or navigates to a recipe

implementation_steps.phase: Initialize React + Vite + Tailwind frontend and FastAPI + SQLAlchemy backend Configure PostgreSQL and run initial migrations for users and refresh_tokens tables Implement POST /api/auth/register with email validation and bcrypt hashing Implement POST /api/auth/login returning JWT access and refresh tokens Implement POST /api/auth/logout and POST /api/auth/refresh Build /login and /register pages with form validation and error display Implement JWT middleware for protecting API routes Set up CORS and rate limiting middleware

Create recipes, ingredients, instructions, and recipe_tags tables and migrations Implement GET/POST /api/recipes and GET/PUT/DELETE /api/recipes/{id} Implement PUT endpoints for ingredients, instructions, and tags Build /recipes/new and /recipes/[id]/edit forms with validation Build recipe detail page with ingredients, instructions, and tag display Implement recipe duplication endpoint and UI action

Implement GET /api/recipes with search, tag filter, favourites, sort, and pagination Build /dashboard with recipe card grid and loading skeleton Implement search input with 300ms debounce Implement tag filter multi-select dropdown Implement favourites toggle filter Implement pagination controls and sort options Implement empty state and no-results state

Implement POST /api/recipes/{id}/image with multipart upload and image resizing Add cover photo upload to the recipe create/edit forms Implement GET /api/tags and POST /api/tags/rename endpoints Build /settings/tags page for tag management Implement serving size scaling control on the recipe detail page Add proportional quantity calculation and reset functionality

Implement responsive design across all breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop) Add dark mode toggle with localStorage persistence Implement toast notification system and confirmation dialogs Persist filter and sort state to URL query parameters Add full keyboard navigation and ARIA labels throughout Write API integration tests for all endpoints (happy paths and key error cases) Write frontend component tests for critical UI flows Ensure test coverage reaches 90% threshold

success_criteria.functionality: All feature bullets implemented and manually verified User can create, edit, duplicate, and delete recipes with all fields Serving size scaling calculates correctly and resets cleanly Tag filtering applies AND logic and URL state persists on refresh Search updates results within 300ms of typing (debounced) Image upload rejects invalid types and oversized files
success_criteria.ux: App is fully usable on iPhone SE (375px width) and up All loading states use skeleton screens — no blank flash All destructive actions have confirmation dialogs Dark mode works across all pages and persists after refresh Keyboard navigation reaches all interactive elements without a mouse
success_criteria.technical_quality: All API endpoints return correct HTTP status codes and envelopes JWT authentication protects all /api/* routes except /api/auth/* Image upload validates type by MIME type and rejects oversized files No N+1 query issues in recipe list endpoint Test coverage >= 90% No TypeScript or mypy type errors No secrets committed to source control
